Valve released the Steam Deck in 2022.
The Steam Deck is a handheld gaming PC running a modified version of Linux.
Valve designed the Steam Deck for portability and broader accessibility.
Valve has been actively developing a native Linux client for Steam from 2012 to the present, initially for Ubuntu.
Valve intended the native Linux client to broaden Steam's reach and appeal to Linux users.
Valve implemented OpenXR support in SteamVR in 2021.
OpenXR support in SteamVR allows developers to create applications compatible with various PC VR hardware.
The Steam Deck is designed to be distro-agnostic.
The Steam Deck works across various PC hardware, unlike the Ubuntu-based Steam Machines.
